Title: A specialized flavone biosynthetic pathway has evolved in the medicinal plant, Scutellaria baicalensis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Single CHI transcript identified in roots; no novel root-specific isoform found
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient expression in tobacco leaves; cinnamic acid supplementation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral activities
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not a specialized determinant of the pathway; endogenous or spontaneous chalcone isomerization is sufficient for pinocembrin/chrysin formation
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq identification; transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana with SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2 ± SbFNSII-2; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Only one CHI transcript was identified, and no novel root-specific CHI isoform was found. 2. In tobacco reconstruction, adding SbCHI did not substantially change pinocembrin production relative to SbCLL-7 plus SbCHS-2 alone. 3. The study concluded that a specialized CHI is not required for the root-specific 4'-deoxyflavone pathway.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not a priority engineering target for this pathway because host CHI or spontaneous isomerization can suffice
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included in pathway reconstruction experiments, but evidence indicates it is not a specialized core determinant compared with SbCLL-7, SbCHS-2, and SbFNSII-2

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; strongly root-enriched versus stems, leaves, and flowers; induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. baicalensis roots; transgenically expressed in Arabidopsis and tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate treatment; pinocembrin feeding; transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts the 4'-deoxyflavanone pinocembrin to the flavone chrysin via NADPH-dependent dehydrogenation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with reported antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral activities
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms an immediate flavone intermediate that is further decorated to produce baicalein, baicalin, wogonin, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of root-specific 4'-deoxyflavones; silencing sharply reduces baicalin, baicalein, wogonin, and wogonoside and causes accumulation of pinocembrin-derived intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNAi silencing in hairy roots; qRT-PCR; metabolite profiling by HPLC/LC-MS-MS; yeast microsome enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; Arabidopsis overexpression with substrate feeding; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SbCLL-7; SbCHS-2; SbCHI; Arabidopsis NADPH cytochrome P450 reductase 1 in WAT11
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbFNSII-2 is root-enriched and MeJA inducible, matching root-specific flavone accumulation. 2. RNAi silencing strongly reduced baicalin, baicalein, wogonoside, and wogonin and caused accumulation of pinocembrin-derived compounds, demonstrating in planta necessity. 3. Recombinant SbFNSII-2 specifically converted pinocembrin to chrysin but did not accept naringenin or eriodictyol. 4. Overexpression in Arabidopsis produced chrysin only when pinocembrin was fed, confirming the required substrate. 5. Together with SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2, SbFNSII-2 reconstituted the core 4'-deoxyflavone pathway in tobacco leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for metabolic engineering of medicinal 4'-deoxyflavones such as chrysin, baicalein, and wogonin when combined with upstream pinocembrin-supplying genes, especially SbCLL-7 and SbCHS-2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires pinocembrin supply; lower catalytic efficiency than SbFNSII-1; no product formation in Arabidopsis without precursor feeding
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Neofunctionalized FNSII isoform specialized for 4'-deoxyflavanone substrate after recent duplication from SbFNSII-1

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Scutellaria baicalensis; Arabidopsis thaliana; Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Preferentially expressed in roots; lower in aerial organs; not significantly induced by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. baicalensis roots; transgenically expressed in Arabidopsis and tobacco leaves; recombinant protein in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; cinnamic acid feeding; transient expression in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Cinnamoyl-CoA supply branch for pinocembrin formation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream branch-entry and precursor activation step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA in an ATP- and CoA-dependent ligation reaction
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral activities
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies the committed precursor pool for pinocembrin and downstream 4'-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes root-specific 4'-deoxyflavone biosynthesis by supplying cinnamoyl-CoA; RNAi reduces baicalin, baicalein, and wogonoside
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR expression profiling; recombinant enzyme assays and kinetics in E. coli; RNAi silencing in hairy roots; metabolite profiling; Arabidopsis overexpression; transient pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCLL-7 is root-enriched and phylogenetically distinct from canonical 4CLs. 2. Recombinant SbCLL-7 specifically used cinnamic acid and showed much higher catalytic efficiency for cinnamic acid than other tested Scutellaria CLLs. 3. RNAi knockdown reduced major root-specific flavones in hairy roots, demonstrating requirement in planta. 4. SbCLL-7 alone did not produce pinocembrin in Arabidopsis, indicating that downstream specialized enzymes are also needed. 5. Coexpression with SbCHS-2 enabled pinocembrin formation and with SbFNSII-2 enabled chrysin formation in tobacco leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical engineering target to redirect flux from cinnamic acid into medicinal 4'-deoxyflavones; should be stacked with SbCHS-2 and SbFNSII-2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with endogenous C4H for cinnamic acid; alone insufficient to generate pinocembrin in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents recruitment of a CLL ancestor likely related to fatty acid metabolism rather than duplication of canonical 4CL

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase, chalcone/stilbene synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots; very low in flowers; induced strongly by MeJA
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In S. baicalensis roots; transiently expressed in tobacco leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: MeJA treatment; cinnamic acid supplementation in tobacco infiltration assays
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses cinnamoyl-CoA with malonyl-CoA units to form the chalcone precursor of pinocembrin
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Bioactive medicinal flavones with antitumor, antioxidant, and antiviral activities
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms the immediate precursor scaffold for pinocembrin and downstream 4'-deoxyflavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for pinocembrin and chrysin formation in reconstructed pathway; root-specific CHS isoform associated with 4'-deoxyflavone production
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq and qRT-PCR expression profiling; transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana with SbCLL-7 ± SbCHI ± SbFNSII-2; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SbCHS-2 is the root-preferential CHS isoform, unlike flower-enriched SbCHS-1. 2. Its expression pattern and MeJA responsiveness are consistent with root-specific flavone biosynthesis. 3. Coexpression of SbCHS-2 with SbCLL-7 in tobacco leaves generated pinocembrin, establishing specialized use of cinnamoyl-CoA. 4. Addition of SbFNSII-2 converted this flux to chrysin, confirming SbCHS-2 as a core enzyme in the root-specific pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential partner gene for engineering pinocembrin and downstream 4'-deoxyflavones; should be combined with SbCLL-7 and SbFNSII-2
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Specialized function inferred mainly from reconstruction and expression correlation rather than direct purified enzyme kinetics in this study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, SRA BioProject PRJNA300475; study SRP068883; sample SRS1263237; experiment SRX1546377; run SRR3123399
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highly similar to previously reported Scutellaria CHS root isoforms, likely allelic with earlier sequences
